name: Tony (Tongyun) Yang
tagline: mean the limits of the world?
in more than we can name.
heroSpecialtyLine: I work on the hard, useful parts of AI. The kind that still works when data is scarce, and stays cheap enough for people to actually use.

paragraphs.lead: I've spent a while exploring all over AI
paragraphs.rest: , from emotion recognition and medical imaging to LLM routing and agent benchmarks. This spring I left my PhD fellowship. It was a hard call, but also a sobering dose, and now I finally know what I want to do next.

AI is shifting from a scaling race to a systems race. Bigger models still matter, but they are not enough. What counts is how we train, route, compress, and ship them. I want AI that is capable, efficient, and genuinely useful.

paragraphs.lead: I'm an independent AI researcher in Amsterdam
paragraphs.rest: , working with industry and academia. Right now I'm waiting for the next opportunity. Prior to this, Marie Skłodowska-Curie Fellow, AI research engineer at TU Delft Imaging Physics, and an MSc from TU Delft.

intro: Open to research, in academia or industry. I care more about whether the work matters than which field it sits in.
currently: Independent AI researcher in Amsterdam, open to what's next.

directions.title: When data is scarce, design matters more
directions.blurb: When data is sensitive or scarce, you can't just scale your way to a good model. The gains come from better architecture and smarter methods. That's the work I enjoy most, building where there isn't much to learn from yet.

directions.title: Frontier performance people can actually use
directions.blurb: You don't always need a frontier model to get frontier performance. I work on reaching that bar with models small and cheap enough that people can actually run them. There's a lot more worth building than another chatbot.

researchDek: The research I've worked on so far.
projectsIntro: Where the research turns into real systems. Some are live collaborations (Gradient Networks, Tencent, MeetaVista). The rest I build on my own time.

businessCard.name: Tony Yang
businessCard.looking: Open to research · academia or industry
businessCard.tagline: Hard problems where data is scarce · frontier performance people can use

contactInvitation: I read every message. Write to me about research, working together, supervision, or just to say hi. I usually reply within a day, and I am easy to talk to.
